page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7se.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the instance features a 48 MB L3 cache and supports hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. It operates on a KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a distinct performance profile. While single-core operations, such as stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core tests, achieve average tier results, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are consistently poor. The instance recorded a multi-core Geekbench score of 1807.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 1933.88, both falling into the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth tests for large blocks also registered in the bottom 10%, with read and write speeds of 14.81 GB/sec and 13.68 GB/sec respectively.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.r7se.large offers a qualitative cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive applications that do not demand high compute throughput. Given the low multi-core processing and database operation scores, this instance is best suited for lightweight memory-bound tasks, single-threaded applications, basic text processing, or small-scale caching layers rather than heavy database servers or parallelized compilation workloads.
